derived.causal_summary: Built the bounded relation-inference backfill that makes packet 019's honest memory_causal_stats stat true. New lib/causal/relation-backfill.ts exports backfillRelationInference(db, { dryRun=true, limit, actor }), mirroring backfillLineageState: it scans bounded memory_index + memory_lineage rows and infers typed causal edges from strong EXISTING deterministic signals only (spec-document chains via the createSpecDocumentChain pairing rules, plus lineage predecessor->successor caused links), all created_by='auto' so they inherit insertEdge's guards (MAX_AUTO_STRENGTH, MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE, window cap, self-loop/orphan rejection), wrapped in a transaction and idempotent via upsert. Default dryRun=true keeps the recovered production DB safe; writes require explicit dryRun:false. After committing it explicitly calls invalidateEntityDensityCache() because raising outgoing-edge counts changes the >=3-outgoing-edge entity-density routing signal that no causal mutation invalidated before. relation-coverage.ts flips backfillJob.implemented=true and sets command to memory_causal_stats({ backfill: { dryRun: false } }); the entry point is wired onto handleMemoryCausalStats (no new MCP tool) with an optional backfill arg added to the strict memoryCausalStatsSchema. Tests prove dryRun-zero-writes, bounded guard-respecting writes + idempotency, entity-density cache invalidation on non-dry runs, and the implemented:true/non-null-command stat flip. Similarity/contradiction signals are deferred best-effort extensions.
